title: Production Refactor Challenge
description: Choose ONE previous FrontForge project and refactor it for production. Improve folder structure, reusable components, custom hooks, performance, accessibility, TypeScript, code quality, error handling, loading states, and responsive design. Then write an architecture discussion defending every decision — no new React concepts, only apply Weeks 1–3 patterns.
requirements: Select exactly ONE project from Weeks 1–3 or Day 22 machine coding submission
Restructure into feature-based folders: components, hooks, api, types per feature
Extract at least two reusable components used in multiple places
Extract at least one custom hook (data fetching, form logic, or UI behavior)
Apply React.memo, useMemo, or useCallback only where Profiler shows benefit — document each
Add lazy loading or code splitting for at least one non-critical route or heavy component
Ensure all async flows have loading, error, and empty states
Accessibility pass: semantic HTML, form labels, keyboard navigation, focus on errors
TypeScript strict: typed props, API models, no any — run npm run build clean
Remove dead code, fix inconsistent naming, enforce single responsibility per file
Responsive layout verified on mobile viewport (375px) and desktop
Write ARCHITECTURE.md or PR description: structure, Context vs Redux vs RTK Query vs Router, trade-offs, three future improvements
acceptanceCriteria: Interviewer can navigate feature folders and find api, types, hooks within 10 seconds
At least two duplicated JSX blocks replaced with shared components
Custom hook is imported and used — not dead code
Performance doc lists each optimization with measure → change → result OR skip reason
Tab through primary flow without mouse — all actions reachable
npm run build passes with zero TypeScript errors
Error state renders user-facing message with retry on at least one API call
Architecture doc answers: why this folder structure, why each state solution, trade-offs, future improvements
Candidate can explain refactor in under 3 minutes out loud
Refactored code pushed to Git with meaningful commit messages
hints: Start with a 15-minute audit: list the top 5 gaps from Day 22 code review before touching code.
Refactor in vertical slices — one feature folder at a time — not a big-bang rewrite.
Extract hooks when you see the same useEffect + useState pattern in two components.
Profile before memo: React DevTools → Profiler → record interaction → find hot components.
Keep original project branch; refactor on a new branch for easy before/after comparison.
Architecture doc is interview prep for Day 24 Round 4 — write it as if presenting to a staff engineer.

bonus.title: Production polish
bonus.description: Only after core refactor acceptance criteria are met.
requirements: Skeleton loaders matching final layout dimensions
Unit tests on one custom hook or pure utility function

debuggingScenarios.title: React.memo Not Working
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Memoized child still re-renders every parent update; Profiler shows no improvement
debuggingScenarios.cause: Parent passes inline object or arrow function as prop — new reference every render defeats shallow compare
debuggingScenarios.fix: useCallback for handlers, useMemo for object props passed to memoized children — or lift state down

debuggingScenarios.title: Context Causes App-Wide Re-renders
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Changing theme re-renders entire component tree including unrelated features
debuggingScenarios.cause: Context provider value is a new object literal every render: value={{ theme, toggle }}
debuggingScenarios.fix: Memoize context value with useMemo; split ThemeContext from AuthContext; consider selectors

debuggingScenarios.title: Lazy Route Shows Blank Screen
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Navigating to lazy-loaded route shows white screen; no error in console
debuggingScenarios.cause: Missing Suspense boundary around lazy component, or incorrect default export in dynamic import
debuggingScenarios.fix: Wrap Routes in Suspense with fallback; ensure lazy(() => import('./X')) targets default export

debuggingScenarios.title: TypeScript Props Mismatch After Extract
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Extracted component shows TS errors; optional props treated as required
debuggingScenarios.cause: Shared component interface does not match all call sites after refactor
debuggingScenarios.fix: Define strict props interface; use optional fields with defaults; fix all call sites before moving on

debuggingScenarios.title: Focus Trap on Error
debuggingScenarios.symptom: After API error, keyboard user cannot reach retry button; focus lost
debuggingScenarios.cause: Error panel rendered without focus management; conditional render unmounts focused element
debuggingScenarios.fix: useRef + useEffect to focus error heading or retry button when error state becomes true

debuggingScenarios.title: Mobile Layout Breaks After Refactor
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Desktop layout fine; mobile shows horizontal scroll and clipped buttons
debuggingScenarios.cause: Fixed widths copied from desktop refactor; flex/grid not adjusted for small viewports
debuggingScenarios.fix: Mobile-first CSS; min-width 0 on flex children; test at 375px in DevTools device mode
